NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “The Silent Dove in Distant Lands.” A Michtam of David when the Philistines captured him in Gath. Be merciful to me, O God, for man would swallow me up; Fighting all day he oppresses me.
NKJV.2: My enemies would hound me all day, For there are many who fight against me, O Most High.
NKJV.3: Whenever I am afraid, I will trust in You.
NKJV.4: In God (I will praise His word), In God I have put my trust;  I will not fear. What can flesh do to me?
NKJV.5: All day they twist my words; All their thoughts are against me for evil.
NKJV.6: They gather together, They hide, they mark my steps, When they lie in wait for my life.
NKJV.7: Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples, O God!
NKJV.8: You number my wanderings; Put my tears into Your bottle; Are they not in Your book?
NKJV.9: When I cry out to You, Then my enemies will turn back; This I know, because God is for me.
NKJV.10: In God (I will praise His word), In the LORD (I will praise His word),
NKJV.11: In God I have put my trust; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?
NKJV.12: Vows made to You are binding upon me, O God; I will render praises to You,
NKJV.13: For You have delivered my soul from death. Have You not kept my feet from falling, That I may walk before God In the light of the living?

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Jonathelemrechokim<S>3128</S>, Michtam<S>4387</S> of David<S>1732</S>, when the Philistines<S>6430</S> took<S>270</S> him in Gath<S>1661</S>. Be merciful<S>2603</S> unto me, O God<S>430</S>: for man<S>582</S> would swallow me up<S>7602</S>; he fighting<S>3898</S> daily<S>3117</S> oppresseth<S>3905</S> me. <sup>Michtam: or, A golden Psalm of David</sup>
KJV.2: Mine enemies<S>8324</S> would daily<S>3117</S> swallow<S>7602</S> me up: for they be many<S>7227</S> that fight<S>3898</S> against me, O thou most High<S>4791</S>. <sup>enemies: Heb. observers</sup>

KJV.4: In God<S>430</S> I will praise<S>1984</S> his word<S>1697</S>, in God<S>430</S> I have put my trust<S>982</S>; I will not fear<S>3372</S> what flesh<S>1320</S> can do<S>6213</S> unto me.
KJV.5: Every day<S>3117</S> they wrest<S>6087</S> my words<S>1697</S>: all their thoughts<S>4284</S> are against me for evil<S>7451</S>.
KJV.6: They gather themselves together<S>1481</S>, they hide<S>6845</S> <S>6845</S> themselves, they mark<S>8104</S> my steps<S>6119</S>, when they wait<S>6960</S> for my soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.7: Shall they escape<S>6405</S> by iniquity<S>205</S>? in thine anger<S>639</S> cast down<S>3381</S> the people<S>5971</S>, O God<S>430</S>.
KJV.8: Thou tellest<S>5608</S> my wanderings<S>5112</S>: put<S>7760</S> thou my tears<S>1832</S> into thy bottle<S>4997</S>: are they not in thy book<S>5612</S>?

KJV.10: In God<S>430</S> will I praise<S>1984</S> his word<S>1697</S>: in the LORD<S>3068</S> will I praise<S>1984</S> his word<S>1697</S>.
KJV.11: In God<S>430</S> have I put my trust<S>982</S>: I will not be afraid<S>3372</S> what man<S>120</S> can do<S>6213</S> unto me.

KJV.13: For thou hast delivered<S>5337</S> my soul<S>5315</S> from death<S>4194</S>: wilt not thou deliver my feet<S>7272</S> from falling<S>1762</S>, that I may walk<S>1980</S> before<S>6440</S> God<S>430</S> in the light<S>216</S> of the living<S>2416</S>?

ESV.1: Be gracious to me, O God, for man tramples on me; all day long an attacker oppresses me;
ESV.2: my enemies trample on me all day long, for many attack me proudly.
ESV.3: When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
ESV.4: In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I shall not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?
ESV.5: All day long they injure my cause;  all their thoughts are against me for evil.
ESV.6: They stir up strife, they lurk; they watch my steps, as they have waited for my life.
ESV.7: For their crime will they escape? In wrath cast down the peoples, O God!
ESV.8: You have kept count of my tossings;  put my tears in your bottle. Are they not in your book?
ESV.9: Then my enemies will turn back in the day when I call. This I know, that  God is for me.
ESV.10: In God, whose word I praise, in the LORD, whose word I praise,
ESV.11: in God I trust; I shall not be afraid. What can man do to me?
ESV.12: I must perform my vows to you, O God; I will render thank offerings to you.
ESV.13: For you have delivered my soul from death, yes, my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of life.

NASB.1: For the choir director; according to Jonath elem rehokim. A Mikhtam of David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be gracious to me, O God, for man has trampled upon me; Fighting all day long he oppresses me.
NASB.2: My foes have trampled upon me all day long, For they are many who fight proudly against me.
NASB.3: When I am afraid, I will put my trust in You.
NASB.4: In God, whose word I praise, In God I have put my trust; I shall not be afraid. What can [mere] man do to me?
NASB.5: All day long they distort my words; All their thoughts are against me for evil.
NASB.6: They attack, they lurk, They watch my steps, As they have waited [to take] my life.
NASB.7: Because of wickedness, cast them forth, In anger put down the peoples, O God!
NASB.8: You have taken account of my wanderings; Put my tears in Your bottle. Are [they] not in Your book?
NASB.9: Then my enemies will turn back in the day when I call; This I know, that God is for me.
NASB.10: In God, [whose] word I praise, In the LORD, [whose] word I praise,
NASB.11: In God I have put my trust, I shall not be afraid. What can man do to me?
NASB.12: Your vows are [binding] upon me, O God; I will render thank offerings to You.
NASB.13: For You have delivered my soul from death, Indeed my feet from stumbling, So that I may walk before God In the light of the living.

NLT.1:
O God, have mercy on me,
for people are hounding me.
My foes attack me all day long.
NLT.2:
I am constantly hounded by those who slander me,
and many are boldly attacking me.
NLT.3:
But when I am afraid,
I will put my trust in you.
NLT.4:
I praise God for what he has promised.
I trust in God, so why should I be afraid?
What can mere mortals do to me?
NLT.5:
They are always twisting what I say;
they spend their days plotting to harm me.
NLT.6:
They come together to spy on me —
watching my every step, eager to kill me.
NLT.7:
Don’t let them get away with their wickedness;
in your anger, O God, bring them down.
NLT.8:
You keep track of all my sorrows.
You have collected all my tears in your bottle.
You have recorded each one in your book.
NLT.9:
My enemies will retreat when I call to you for help.
This I know: God is on my side!
NLT.10:
I praise God for what he has promised;
yes, I praise the LORD for what he has promised.
NLT.11:
I trust in God, so why should I be afraid?
What can mere mortals do to me?
NLT.12:
I will fulfill my vows to you, O God,
and will offer a sacrifice of thanks for your help.
NLT.13:
For you have rescued me from death;
you have kept my feet from slipping.
So now I can walk in your presence, O God,
in your life-giving light.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Silent Dove in Distant Lands.” A poem by David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be merciful to me, God, for man wants to swallow me up. All day long, he attacks and oppresses me.
WEB.2: My enemies want to swallow me up all day long, for they are many who fight proudly against me.
WEB.3: When I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.
WEB.4: In God, I praise his word. In God, I put my trust. I will not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?
WEB.5: All day long they twist my words. All their thoughts are against me for evil.
WEB.6: They conspire and lurk, watching my steps, they are eager to take my life.
WEB.7: Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples, God.
WEB.8: You count my wanderings. You put my tears into your bottle. Aren’t they in your book?
WEB.9: Then my enemies shall turn back in the day that I call. I know this, that God is for me.
WEB.10: In God, I will praise his word. In Yahweh, I will praise his word.
WEB.11: I have put my trust in God. I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?
WEB.12: Your vows are on me, God. I will give thank offerings to you.
WEB.13: For you have delivered my soul from death, and prevented my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: according to “A Silent Dove Far Away.” A of David. When the Philistines seized him in Gath. ⓜ [1] Be gracious to me, God, for a man is trampling me;
he fights and oppresses me all day long.
CSB17.2:
My adversaries trample me all day,
for many arrogantly fight against me. [1] ⓝ
CSB17.3:
When I am afraid,
I will trust in you. ⓐ
CSB17.4:
In God, whose word I praise,
in God I trust; I will not be afraid.
What can mere mortals do to me? ⓑ
CSB17.5:
They twist my words all day long;
all their thoughts against me are evil. ⓒ
CSB17.6:
They stir up strife, [2] they lurk;
they watch my steps
while they wait to take my life. ⓓ
CSB17.7:
Will they escape in spite of such sin?
God, bring down the nations in wrath. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
You yourself have recorded my wanderings. [3]
Put my tears in your bottle.
Are they not in your book? ⓕ
CSB17.9:
Then my enemies will retreat on the day when I call. ⓖ
This I know: God is for me. ⓗ
CSB17.10:
In God, whose word I praise,
in the LORD, whose word I praise,
CSB17.11:
in God I trust; I will not be afraid.
What can mere humans do to me? ⓘ
CSB17.12:
I am obligated by vows [4] to you, God;
I will make my thank offerings to you. ⓙ
CSB17.13:
For you rescued me from death,
even my feet from stumbling,
to walk before God in the light of life. ⓚ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] "Silent Dove Among Those Far Away." A Mikhtam of David. [A record of memorable thoughts] when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be gracious to me, O God, for man has trampled on me;
All day long the adversary oppresses and torments me.
AMP.2:
My enemies have trampled upon me all day long,
For they are many who fight proudly against me.
AMP.3:
When I am afraid,
I will put my trust and faith in You.
AMP.4:
In God, whose word I praise;
In God I have put my trust;
I shall not fear.
What can mere man do to me?
AMP.5:
All day long they twist my words and say hurtful things;
All their thoughts are against me for evil.
AMP.6:
They attack, they hide and lurk,
They watch my steps,
As they have [expectantly] waited to take my life.
AMP.7:
Cast them out because of their wickedness.
In anger bring down the peoples, O God!
AMP.8:
You have taken account of my wanderings;
Put my tears in Your bottle.
Are they not recorded in Your book?
AMP.9:
Then my enemies will turn back in the day when I call;
This I know, that God is for me.
AMP.10:
In God, whose word I praise,
In the LORD, whose word I praise,
AMP.11:
In God have I put my trust and confident reliance; I will not be afraid.
What can man do to me?
AMP.12:
Your vows are binding upon me, O God;
I will give thank offerings to You.
AMP.13:
For You have rescued my soul from death,
Yes, and my feet from stumbling,
So that I may walk before God
In the light of life.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, when he was captured by the Philistines in Gath.
Take my side, God—I'm getting kicked around,
stomped on every day.
MSG.2:
Not a day goes by
but somebody beats me up;
They make it their duty
to beat me up.
MSG.3:
When I get really afraid
I come to you in trust.
MSG.4:
I'm proud to praise God;
fearless now, I trust in God.
What can mere mortals do?
MSG.5:
They don't let up—
they smear my reputation
and huddle to plot my collapse.
MSG.6:
They gang up,
sneak together through the alleys
To take me by surprise,
wait their chance to get me.
MSG.7:
Pay them back in evil!
Get angry, God!
Down with these people!
MSG.8:
You've kept track of my every toss and turn
through the sleepless nights,
Each tear entered in your ledger,
each ache written in your book.
MSG.9:
If my enemies run away,
turn tail when I yell at them,
Then I'll know
that God is on my side.
MSG.10:
I'm proud to praise God,
proud to praise God.
MSG.11:
Fearless now, I trust in God;
what can mere mortals do to me?
MSG.12:
God, you did everything you promised,
and I'm thanking you with all my heart.
MSG.13:
You pulled me from the brink of death,
my feet from the cliff-edge of doom.
Now I stroll at leisure with God
in the sunlit fields of life.

NIV.1:
Psalm 56
For the director of music. To the tune of “A Dove on Distant Oaks.” Of David. A miktam. When the Philistines had seized him in Gath.
Be merciful to me, O God, for men hotly pursue me;
all day long they press their attack.
NIV.2:
My slanderers pursue me all day long;
many are attacking me in their pride.
NIV.3:
When I am afraid,
I will trust in you.
NIV.4:
In God, whose word I praise,
in God I trust; I will not be afraid.
What can mortal man do to me?
NIV.5:
All day long they twist my words;
they are always plotting to harm me.
NIV.6:
They conspire, they lurk,
they watch my steps,
eager to take my life.
NIV.7:
On no account let them escape;
in your anger, O God, bring down the nations.
NIV.8:
Record my lament;
list my tears on your scroll—
are they not in your record?
NIV.9:
Then my enemies will turn back
when I call for help.
By this I will know that God is for me.
NIV.10:
In God, whose word I praise,
in the Lord, whose word I praise—
NIV.11:
in God I trust; I will not be afraid.
What can man do to me?
NIV.12:
I am under vows to you, O God;
I will present my thank offerings to you.
NIV.13:
For you have delivered me from death
and my feet from stumbling,
that I may walk before God
in the light of life.

NRSVCE.1:
Be gracious to me, O God, for people trample on me;
all day long foes oppress me;
NRSVCE.2:
my enemies trample on me all day long,
for many fight against me.
O Most High,
NRSVCE.3:
when I am afraid,
I put my trust in you.
NRSVCE.4:
In God, whose word I praise,
in God I trust; I am not afraid;
what can flesh do to me?
NRSVCE.5:
All day long they seek to injure my cause;
all their thoughts are against me for evil.
NRSVCE.6:
They stir up strife, they lurk,
they watch my steps.
As they hoped to have my life,
NRSVCE.7:
so repay them for their crime;
in wrath cast down the peoples, O God!
NRSVCE.8:
You have kept count of my tossings;
put my tears in your bottle.
Are they not in your record?
NRSVCE.9:
Then my enemies will retreat
in the day when I call.
This I know, that God is for me.
NRSVCE.10:
In God, whose word I praise,
in the LORD, whose word I praise,
NRSVCE.11:
in God I trust; I am not afraid.
What can a mere mortal do to me?
NRSVCE.12:
My vows to you I must perform, O God;
I will render thank offerings to you.
NRSVCE.13:
For you have delivered my soul from death,
and my feet from falling,
so that I may walk before God
in the light of life.

NET.1: Have mercy on me, O God, for men are attacking me! All day long hostile enemies are tormenting me.
NET.2: Those who anticipate my defeat attack me all day long. Indeed, many are fighting against me, O Exalted One.
NET.3: When I am afraid, I trust in you.
NET.4: In God — I boast in his promise — in God I trust, I am not afraid. What can mere men do to me?
NET.5: All day long they cause me trouble; they make a habit of plotting my demise.
NET.6: They stalk and lurk; they watch my every step, as they prepare to take my life.
NET.7: Because they are bent on violence, do not let them escape! In your anger bring down the nations, O God!
NET.8: You keep track of my misery. Put my tears in your leather container! Are they not recorded in your scroll?
NET.9: My enemies will turn back when I cry out to you for help; I know that God is on my side.
NET.10: In God — I boast in his promise — in the LORD — I boast in his promise —
NET.11: in God I trust, I am not afraid. What can mere men do to me?
NET.12: I am obligated to fulfill the vows I made to you, O God; I will give you the thank-offerings you deserve,
NET.13: when you deliver my life from death. You keep my feet from stumbling, so that I might serve God as I enjoy life.
